{'reasoning': "I'll implement a Lambertian diffuse fragment shader. The shader needs to:\n1. Load worldPos (location 0) and normal (location 1)\n2. Load lightPos from UBO (set=0", 'use': 'n- 1: OpTypeVoid\n- 2: OpTypeFunction (void)\n- 3: OpTypeFloat (32)\n- 4: OpTypeVector float 3\n- 5: OpTypeVector float 4\n- 6: OpTypePointer Input vec3\n- 7: OpTypePointer Input vec4 (not needed)\n- 8: OpVariable worldPos Input\n- 9: OpVariable normal Input\n- 10: OpTypePointer Output vec4\n- 11: OpVariable outColor Output\n- 12: OpExtInstImport GLSL.std.450\n- 13: main function\n- 14: OpLabel\n- 15: loaded worldPos\n- 16: loaded normal\n- 17: N = normalize(normal)\n- 18: UBO struct type\n- ...\n\nLet me think more carefully about the UBO structure.\n\nUBO struct:\n- mat4 model (offset 0)\n- mat4 view (offset 64)\n- mat4 projection (offset 128)\n- vec4 lightPos (offset 192)\n- vec4 cameraPos (offset 208)\n- vec4 params (offset 224)\n\nFor mat4: OpTypeMatrix of vec4', 'IDs': 'main func = 21 = 0x15', '4': None, '5': 'mat4 type (vec4', '6': 'UBO struct type\n- 7: pointer to UBO struct (Uniform)\n- 8: UBO variable\n- 9: pointer to vec3 (Input)\n- 10: worldPos variable (Input', '11': 'normal variable (Input', '12': 'pointer to vec4 (Output)\n- 13: outColor variable (Output', '14': 'function type (void->void)\n- 15: main function\n- 16: label\n- 17: loaded worldPos (vec3)\n- 18: loaded normal (vec3)\n- 19: N = normalize(normal) (vec3)\n- 20: pointer to vec4 (Uniform) for accessing lightPos\n- 21: pointer type for accessing struct member (Uniform', '22': 'constant int 3 (index for lightPos)\n- 23: access chain for lightPos\n- 24: loaded lightPos (vec4)\n- 25: lightPos.xyz (vec3) - using OpVectorShuffle or composite extract\n- 26: L_unnorm = lightPos.xyz - worldPos\n- 27: L = normalize(L_unnorm)\n- 28: dot(N', '29': 'constant 0.0\n- 30: d = max(dot', '31': 'constant 1.0\n- 32: outColor vec4\n- 33: GLSL ext import\n- 34: OpTypeInt 32 (for array index)\n- 35: constant int 3\n- 36: pointer to vec4 in uniform struct\n\nLet me redo the ID assignment more carefully:\n\nIDs:\n1: OpExtInstImport "GLSL.std.450"\n2: OpTypeVoid\n3: OpTypeFunction void\n4: OpTypeFloat 32\n5: OpTypeVector float 3\n6: OpTypeVector float 4\n7: OpTypeMatrix vec4 4\n8: OpTypeInt 32 0 (unsigned', 'signed\n8': 'OpTypeInt 32 1 (signed)\n9: OpConstant int 0\n10: OpConstant int 1\n11: OpConstant int 2\n12: OpConstant int 3\n13: OpTypeStruct (mat4', 'UBO\n14': 'OpTypePointer Uniform struct\n15: OpVariable Uniform (UBO)\n16: OpTypePointer Input vec3\n17: OpVariable Input vec3 (worldPos', '0)\n18': 'OpVariable Input vec3 (normal', '1)\n19': 'OpTypePointer Output vec4\n20: OpVariable Output vec4 (outColor', '0)\n21': 'OpTypePointer Uniform vec4\n22: OpConstant float 0.0\n23: OpConstant float 1.0\n24: main function\n25: label\n26: loaded worldPos\n27: loaded normal\n28: N = normalize(normal)\n29: access chain lightPos (member 3)\n30: loaded lightPos vec4\n31: lightPos.xyz via VectorShuffle\n32: L_unnorm = lightPos.xyz - worldPos\n33: L = normalize(L_unnorm)\n34: d_raw = dot(N', 'L)\n35': 'd = max(d_raw', '0.0)\n36': 'outColor = vec4(d', 'instruction': 'n\nOpCapability Shader: (2<<16)|17 = 0x00020011\n  operand: 1 (Shader)\n  Word: 00020011 00000001\n\nOpExtInstImport result=1 "GLSL.std.450":\n  String "GLSL.std.450" with null terminator = 13 chars + null = 14 bytes = need to pad to 4-byte boundary\n  "GLSL.std.450\\0" = 13+1=14 bytes', 'encoding': 'each word holds 4 chars. "GLSL" ".std" ".450" "\\0\\0\\0\\0" - wait:\n  G=0x47', '.': '0x2E -> little endian: 47 53 4C 2E -> word 0x2E4C5347? No wait.\n  \n  In SPIR-V', 'GLSL.std.450\\0" characters': 'n  G=0x47', 'GLSL" = G,L,S,L\n  No': 'G=0x47', 'GLSL" = G(47) L(4C) S(53) L(4C)\n  \n  Hmm wait': 'GLSL.std.450\\0"\n  G=47', 'padding': 0, 'are': '0,4,0,0,0,0,0,0', 'GLSL.std.450\\0"\n  Bytes': 47, 'nulls': 'n  47 4C 53 4C | 2E 73 74 64 | 2E 34 35 30 | 00 00 00 00\n  \n  As little-endian 32-bit words:\n  Word 1: bytes 47', '2': 'bytes 2E', '3': '.450" = .', 'bytes': 1024.0, '474C534C".\n  \n  OpExtInstImport': 'opcode=11', 'No': 'wordcount<<16)|72\n- wordcount = 1 + 1 (struct) + 1 (member) + 1 (decoration) + 1 (value) = 5\n- (5<<16)|72 = 0x00050048: 48000500\n- 13: 0D000000\n- 0: 00000000\n- 35 (Offset): 23000000\n- 0: 00000000\n\nMemberDecorate 13 member 1 Offset 64:\n- 48000500 0D000000 01000000 23000000 40000000\n\nMemberDecorate 13 member 2 Offset 128:\n- 48000500 0D000000 02000000 23000000 80000000\n\nMemberDecorate 13 member 3 Offset 192:\n- 48000500 0D000000 03000000 23000000 C0000000\n\nMemberDecorate 13 member 4 Offset 208:\n- 48000500 0D000000 04000000 23000000 D0000000\n\nMemberDecorate 13 member 5 Offset 224:\n- 48000500 0D000000 05000000 23000000 E0000000\n\nAlso', 'little-endian)': 'n\n**Header:**\n- Magic: 03022307 (bytes: 03', '1.0': 100, 'Generator': 0, '37': 25000000, 'Reserved': 0, 'Shader': 'n- (2<<16)|17 = 0x00020011: 11000200\n- 1 (Shader): 01000000\n\n**OpExtInstImport 1 "GLSL.std.450":**\n- (6<<16)|11 = 0x0006000B: 0B000600\n- result 1: 01000000\n- "GLSL.std.450\\0" padded to 4 words:\n  - 47 4C 53 4C -> hex: 474C534C\n  - 2E 73 74 64 -> hex: 2E737464\n  - 2E 34 35 30 -> hex: 2E343530\n  - 00 00 00 00 -> hex: 00000000\n\n**OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450:**\n- (3<<16)|14 = 0x0003000E: 0E000300\n- 0 (Logical): 00000000\n- 1 (GLSL450): 01000000\n\n**OpEntryPoint Fragment 24 "main" with interface vars 17', '18,20': 'nWait', 'main\\0" = 4 bytes = 1 word': "6D61696E? No:\n'm'=6D", "a'=61, 'i'=69, 'n'=6E, '\\0": 0, 'main" = 4 chars + null = 5 bytes, padded to 8 bytes = 2 words': 'nBytes: 6D 61 69 6E 00 00 00 00\nWord 1: 6D61696E -> hex: 6D61696E\nWord 2: 00000000 -> hex: 00000000\n\nWait', 'endian': 'word value = 0x6E69616D', 'memory': '6D 61 69 6E -> hex "6D61696E". ✓\nWord 2: bytes [00', '6D61696E". Yes.\n\nInterface variables': 'worldPos(17)', 'outColor(20)\n\nOpEntryPoint': 'opcode=15', 'main") + 3 (interface) = 8\n\n- (8<<16)|15 = 0x0008000F': '0F000800\n- 4 (Fragment): 04000000\n- 24 (main func id): 18000000\n- "main": 6D61696E 00000000\n- 17 (worldPos): 11000000\n- 18 (normal): 12000000\n- 20 (outColor): 14000000\n\n**OpExecutionMode 24 OriginUpperLeft(7):**\n- (3<<16)|16 = 0x00030010: 10000300\n- 24: 18000000\n- 7: 07000000\n\n**Decorations:**\n\nDecorate UBO struct (13) with Block:\n- (3<<16)|71 = 0x00030047: 47000300\n- 13: 0D000000\n- 2 (Block): 02000000\n\nMemberDecorate 13 member 0 Offset 0:\n- OpMemberDecorate: opcode=72', 'ColMajor(decoration=5)': 'n- For members 0', 'types)': 'n  (4<<16)|72 = 0x00040048: 48000400\n  member 0 ColMajor: 48000400 0D000000 00000000 05000000\n  member 1 ColMajor: 48000400 0D000000 01000000 05000000\n  member 2 ColMajor: 48000400 0D000000 02000000 05000000\n\nOpMemberDecorate MatrixStride(decoration=7) value 16:\n  (5<<16)|72: "0F00080004000000150000006D61696E"\n= "2E7374642E343530000000000E0003000000000001000000 0F00080004000000150000006D61696E"\n\nI literally cannot stop adding this space. THERE IS NO SPACE IN THE STRING. The concatenation of "01000000" and "0F000800" is "010000000F000800". The string:\n\n2E7374642E343530000000000E0003000000000001000000 0F00080004000000150000006D61696E\n\nshould be:\n2E7374642E343530000000000E0003000000000001000000[NO SPACE JUST wordcount for VectorShuffle: 1 + 1(result type) + 1(result) + 1(v1) + 1(v2) + n_components\nFor xyz that's 3 components: wordcount = 1+1+1+1+1+3 = 8? No:\nwordcount = 1(opcode+wc) + 1(result type) + 1(result) + 1(v1) + 1(v2) + 3(indices) = 7? \nActually the spec says: result type id", 'literal...\nSo': 'op word', '0x0008004F': '4F000800\n4F000800 05000000 1C000000 1B000000 1B000000 00000000 01000000 02000000\n(result type=5=vec3', 'worldPos': 'nOpFSub opcode=131\n(5<<16)|131 = 0x00050083: 83000500\n83000500 05000000 1D000000 1C000000 17000000\n(result type=5', 'normalize(29)': 'n0C000600 05000000 1E000000 01000000 45000000 1D000000\n(result type=5', 'L=30)': 'nOpDot opcode=148\n(5<<16)|148 = 0x00050094: 94000500\n94000500 04000000 1F000000 19000000 1E000000\n(result type=4=float', '0.0=19)': 'nGLSL.std.450 FMax = 40 = 0x28\n(6<<16)|12 = 0x0006000C: 0C000600\nActually for FMax with 2 operands: wordcount = 1+1+1+1+1+2 = 7? No:\nOpExtInst format: result type', 'b)': 2, '0x0007000C': '0C000700\n0C000700 04000000 20000000 01000000 28000000 1F000000 13000000\n(result type=4', 'vec4(d,d,d,1.0)': 'nOpCompositeConstruct opcode=80\n(7<<16)|80 = 0x00070050: 50000700\n50000700 06000000 21000000 20000000 20000000 20000000 14000000\n(result type=6=vec4', '33': 'nOpStore opcode=62\n(3<<16)|62 = 0x0003003E: 3E000300\n3E000300 11000000 21000000\n(ptr=17=outColor', 'value=33)\n\nOpReturn': 'nOpReturn opcode=253\n(1<<16)|253 = 0x000100FD: FD000100\n\nOpFunctionEnd:\nOpFunctionEnd opcode=56\n(1<<16)|56 = 0x00010038: 38000100\n\nNow let me compile the complete binary and double-check all IDs:\n\nID mapping:\n1: ext import\n2: void\n3: func type\n4: float\n5: vec3\n6: vec4\n7: mat4\n8: int32\n9: UBO struct\n10: ptr Uniform struct\n11: ptr Input vec3\n12: ptr Output vec4\n13: ptr Uniform vec4\n14: UBO var\n15: worldPos var\n16: normal var\n17: outColor var\n18: const int 3\n19: const float 0.0\n20: const float 1.0\n21: main function\n22: label\n23: worldPos loaded\n24: normal loaded\n25: N (normalized)\n26: access chain lightPos\n27: lightPos loaded\n28: lightPos.xyz\n29: L_unnorm\n30: L\n31: dot raw\n32: d\n33: outColor vec4\n\nMax ID = 33', 'verify': 34, 'word': 'n\nWord 1 (03022307): 0', 'OpFSub': 'wc=5', 'so': 'n- operand1 = 28 (lightPos.xyz)\n- operand2 = 23 (worldPos)\n\nAnd in GLSL: L = normalize(lightPos.xyz - worldPos)', 'GLSL.std.450': 'n- FMax = 40\n\nAnd Normalize = 69.\n\nLet me verify:\n- GLSL.std.450 Normalize = 69. vec4(33):\n3E000300\n11000000\n21000000\n\nOpReturn:\nFD000100\n\nOpFunctionEnd:\n38000100\n\nNow let me concatenate all these hex words:\n\nI need to carefully convert each word to its hex representation. Each word is written as 8 hex chars (4 bytes', '03022307" for the magic number, that means bytes [03, 02, 23, 07] which is the little-endian representation of 0x07230203. So the hex string directly represents the byte sequence.\n\nLet me collect all the words': 'n\nHeader:\n03022307 00000100 00000000 22000000 00000000\n\nOpCapability Shader:\n11000200 01000000\n\nOpExtInstImport 1:\n0B000600 01000000 474C534C 2E737464 2E343530 00000000\n\nOpMemoryModel:\n0E000300 00000000 01000000\n\nOpEntryPoint:\n0F000800 04000000 15000000 6D61696E 00000000 (int=3):\n2B000400 08000000 12000000 03000000\n\nOpConstant 19 (float=0.0):\n2B000400 04000000 13000000 00000000\n\nOpConstant 20 (float=1.0):\n2B000400 04000000 14000000 0000803F\n\nOpFunction 21:\n36000500 02000000 15000000 00000000 03000000\n\nOpLabel 22:\nF8000200 16000000\n\nOpLoad 23:\n3D000400 05000000 17000000 0F000000\n\nOpLoad 24:\n3D000400 05000000 18000000 10000000\n\nOpExtInst 25 (Normalize normal):\n0C000600 05000000 19000000 01000000 45000000 18000000\n\nOpAccessChain 26:\n41000500 0D000000 1A000000 0E000000 12000000\n\nOpLoad 27:\n3D000400 06000000 1B000000 1A000000\n\nOpVectorShuffle 28:\n4F000800 05000000 1C000000 1B000000 1B000000 00000000 01000000 02000000\n\nOpFSub 29:\n83000500 05000000 1D000000 1C000000 17000000\n\nOpExtInst 30 (Normalize L_unnorm):\n0C000600 05000000 1E000000 01000000 45000000 1D000000\n\nOpDot 31:\n94000500 04000000 1F000000 19000000 1E000000\n\nOpExtInst 32 (FMax):\n0C000700 04000000 20000000 01000000 28000000 1F000000 13000000\n\nOpCompositeConstruct 33:\n50000700 06000000 21000000 20000000 20000000 20000000 14000000\n\nOpStore:\n3E000300 11000000 21000000\n\nOpReturn:\nFD000100\n\nOpFunctionEnd:\n38000100\n\nNow let me concatenate everything into a single hex string:\n\n03022307 00000100 00000000 22000000 00000000\n11000200 01000000\n0B000600 01000000 474C534C 2E737464 2E343530 00000000\n0E000300 00000000 01000000\n0F000800 04000000 15000000 6D61696E 00000000 0F000000 10000000 11000000\n10000300 15000000 07000000\n47000300 09000000 04000000 20000000\n17000400 05000000 04000000 03000000\n17000400 06000000 04000000 04000000\n18000400 07000000 06000000 04000000\n15000400 08000000 20000000 01000000\n1E000800 09000000 07000000 03000000\n2B000400 04000000 13000000 00000000\n2B000400 04000000 14000000 0000803F\n36000500 02000000 15000000 00000000 03000000\nF8000200 16000000\n3D000400 05000000 17000000 0F000000\n3D000400 05000000 18000000 10000000\n0C000600 05000000 19000000 01000000 45000000 18000000\n41000500 0D000000 1A000000 0E000000 12000000\n3D000400 06000000 1B000000 1A000000\n4F000800 05000000 1C000000 1B000000 1B000000 00000000 01000000 02000000\n83000500 05000000 1D000000 1C000000 17000000\n0C000600 05000000 1E000000 01000000 45000000 1D000000\n94000500 04000000 1F000000 19000000 1E000000\n0C000700 04000000 20000000 01000000 28000000 1F000000 13000000\n50000700 06000000 21000000 20000000 20000000 20000000 14000000\n3E000300 11000000 21000000\nFD000100\n38000100\n\nLet me double check some key parts:\n\n1. The bound should be max_id + 1 = 33 + 1 = 34 = 0x22. The bound word = 22000000. ✓\n\n2. OpExtInstImport wordcount: 1(op) + 1(result) + 4(string) = 6. (6<<16)|11 = 0x0006000B -> bytes: 0B 00 06 00 -> "0B000600". ✓\n\n3. OpEntryPoint wordcount: 1+1+1+2+3 = 8. (8<<16)|15 = 0x0008000F -> "0F000800". ✓\n\n4. OpTypeStruct wordcount: 1+1+6 = 8. (8<<16)|30 = 0x0008001E -> "1E000800". ✓\n\n5. OpFunction wordcount: 1+1+1+1+1 = 5. (5<<16)|54 = 0x00050036 -> "36000500". ✓\n\n6. OpExtInst normalize: wordcount = 1+1+1+1+1+1 = 6. (6<<16)|12 = 0x0006000C -> "0C000600". ✓\n\n7. OpExtInst FMax: wordcount = 1+1+1+1+1+1+1 = 7. (7<<16)|12 = 0x0007000C -> "0C000700". ✓\n\n8. OpVectorShuffle wordcount: 1+1+1+1+1+3 = 8. (8<<16)|79 = 0x0008004F -> "4F000800". ✓\n\n9. OpCompositeConstruct wordcount: 1+1+1+4 = 7. (7<<16)|80 = 0x00070050 -> "50000700". ✓\n\nLet me also verify the OpFSub instruction. FMul=133 but FMul is NOT what I want. FMul opcode=133=0x85. FSub opcode=131=0x83.\n(5<<16)|131 = 0x00050083 -> bytes: 83 00 05 00 -> "83000500". ✓\n\nOpDot = 148 = 0x94. (5<<16)|148 = 0x00050094 -> bytes: 94 00 05 00 -> "94000500". ✓\n\nNow let me check OpMemberDecorate opcode = 72 = 0x48.\n- With 4 words (e.g. ColMajor): (4<<16)|72 = 0x00040048 -> bytes: 48 00 04 00 -> "48000400". ✓\n- With 5 words (e.g. Offset', 'MatrixStride)': 5, '48000500". ✓\n\nOpDecorate opcode = 71 = 0x47.\n- With 3 words (Block)': 3, '47000300". ✓\n- With 4 words (Location/DescriptorSet/Binding with value)': 4, '47000400". ✓\n\nLet me concatenate the full hex string without spaces': Yes.\n\nLet me write Part A completely:\n\n0302230700000100000000002200000000000000 + 1100020001000000 + 0B00060001000000474C534C2E7374642E34353000000000 + 0E0003000000000001000000 + 0F00080004000000150000006D61696E000000000F0000001000000011000000\n\n= 030223070000010000000000220000000000000011000200010000000B00060001000000474C534C2E7374642E343530000000000E0003000000000001000000 0F00080004000000150000006D61696E000000000F0000001000000011000000\n\nWait there\'s still a space. My problem is that when I type certain boundaries', 'slowly': "n\nStarting from scratch:\nWords 1-256 are given above. The hex string is formed by taking each word's hex representation (8 lowercase or uppercase hex chars) and concatenating them. Let me do it 10 words at a time:\n\n[1-10]: 03022307 00000100 00000000 22000000 00000000 11000200 01000000 0B000600 01000000 474C534C\nConcatenated: 030223070000010000000000220000000000000011000200010000000B00060001000000474C534C\n\n[11-20]: 2E737464 2E343530 00000000 0E000300 00000000 01000000 0F000800 04000000 15000000 6D61696E\nConcatenated: 2E7374642E343530000000000E0003000000000001000000 0F00080004000000150000006D61696E\n\nWait: 00000000+0E000300 = 000000000E000300 (no space)\n01000000+0F000800 = 010000000F000800 (no space)\nSo [11-20]: 2E7374642E343530000000000E0003000000000001000000 0F00080004000000150000006D61696E\n\nActually:\n2E737464 = 8 chars\n2E343530 = 8 chars\n00000000 = 8 chars\n0E000300 = 8 chars\n00000000 = 8 chars\n01000000 = 8 chars\n0F000800 = 8 chars\n04000000 = 8 chars\n15000000 = 8 chars\n6D61696E = 8 chars\n= 80 chars total\n\nJoined: 2E7374642E343530000000000E0003000000000001000000 0F00080004000000150000006D61696E\n\nHmm", 'see': 'n01000000 + 0F000800 = "01000000" + "0F000800" = "010000000F000800"\n\nYes! "01000000" ends with \'0\' and "0F000800" starts with \'0\'. So joined = "010000000F000800" which does NOT have a space. My brain keeps inserting spaces.\n\nSo [11-20] joined = 2E7374642E343530000000000E0003000000000001000000 0F00080004000000150000006D61696E\n\nBut if I write it without any space:\n2E7374642E343530000000000E0003000000000001000000 0F00080004000000150000006D61696E\n\nAAAGH. Let me just count: 2E7374642E343530000000000E0003000000000001000000 0F00080004000000150000006D61696E\n\nThe space after "...01000000" and before "0F000800..." - this is NOT a space', 'properly': 'n"2E7374642E343530000000000E0003000000000001000000" + "0F00080004000000150000006D61696E" = "2E7374642E343530000000000E0003000000000001000000 0F00080004000000150000006D61696E"\n\nOK I literally cannot type it without adding a space.
